destinations.evidence: Missing and deactivated executors read inactive, active queries filter by is_active, and stats derive active/inactive counts from the current registry state.

equivalence_argument: The registry active-state APIs derive all query and stats results directly from ExecutorInfo.is_active and return false for missing executors.
rationale: The commit adds explicit activate/deactivate state transitions, active membership filtering, and registry statistics intended to expose the current executor set. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: ExecutorRegistry active-state APIs must treat missing executors and deactivated executors as inactive, include only active executors in get_active_executors, and report total, active, and inactive counts consistently in stats.
suggested_falsification_tests: Register two executors, deactivate one, then assert is_executor_active returns true only for the active executor, false for the inactive and an unknown address, get_active_executors returns exactly one entry, and stats reports total 2, active 1, inactive 1.
summary: Registry active queries reflect executor state.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Register two executors, deactivate one, and assert active lookup, active list, and stats report one active and one inactive while unknown addresses are inactive.
title: Registry active queries reflect executor state
